reasoning: Carlsen has been #1 since July 2011 and possesses a substantial 23-point lead over #2 as of late 2025. FIDE lists update monthly, meaning the November 16 date will be determined by the already secure November list. For December 2025 and February 2026, Carlsen's low frequency of classical play inherently minimizes rating loss risks, while challengers like Nakamura (#2) would struggle to gain 20+ points in such a short timeframe. He is well within the 1-year inactivity limit, assuring he remains active in the rankings.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Magnus Carlsen have a FIDE ranking on each resolution date listed below as high or higher than their ranking on 2025-11-09?
